mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing equity valuations to decline over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 6.0%, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and decreases in consumer prices indicate strong real demand without inflationary pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by >2%, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding corporate output capacity and rising earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real gross domestic product reflects broad economic strength and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >65% of periods following inflation spikes above the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical edge with t-stat below 1.5 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k but UNRATE remains flat, or interaction effect vanishes with p > 0.15 in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate renewed confidence in long-term economic growth and credit availability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days during credit expansion phases.
failure_condition: Housing starts acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day returns during credit expansions, or the signal information coefficient drops below 0.02 in rolling 2-year windows.
